payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Uh, not to your knowledge. It's similar to the previous times that she's been unable to keep her form there for you. Especially between planes, you can imagine it's been rather difficult for her to be here for the time she has.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. Can we see if the door shut?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You look back over your shoulder and notice the door just (creaking, bang) and closes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: --there's a sudden flash of wind, and there's a flicker in Pike's form as all of a sudden she (flickering sound)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And her divine form vanishes in place, apparently sent back to the source of where she projected her essence.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She didn't get, like, stolen or something?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You travel for about another hour or so forward, and you, just being naturally alert as you are, notice some shift of movement that is more sudden than the slow sway of some of the various branches and boughs of nearby trees. A little bit in the direction you're traveling and to the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's a lot, coming from Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How about you, I'll put you on my shoulder, but how about you sit on my shoulder like strapped back there and just hang out the back?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, don't put your feet near my face, like hang out on the back of me.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, all right. Thank you for the lift.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's good, you can keep an eye out for the hag following behind us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, am I like being Yoda-carried right now? (All laugh.)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm flipping and climbing vines and shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man, that makes me want a Scanlan backpack. Oh man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you guys push on forward, Keyleth guiding the direction, you're the only one who moves without an issue. Are you tracking ahead of the party, or are you staying with them?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I am tracking a little bit ahead of them.

payload.speaker: MATT

You now see there are two figures that are facing each other, and they themselves are covered in this horrible liquid, and you recognize the form a little bit. These, they're humanoid, but they have long, exaggerated jaws, very long forearm limbs, tall, straggly black hair, long, pointed hooked nose, and they're both eating something that's partially stuck in this bog. These are trolls, these are two large trolls that are currently in the process of eating some sort of creature.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm gonna try and scope it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Make a perception check with advantage.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, you guys can see, since you've all made note of these creatures in the distance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: -- has been more sensitive, defensive, and more intelligent.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They could be, like, hag trolls, which is just terrifying.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Which could be us very soon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Which could be us very soon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The longer we stand still, the more stuck we get.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear this (grunt) in the distance, and you now, looking through your scope, you see that one of them has looked up from its eating, its mouth covered in all sorts of shiny, fresh liquid, and it kind of glances past. payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes you a minute, but you manage to get up to the tree. You come up into the trees. Do you break the canopy, or do you stop up at the branches?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Turns around, (trollish grunt), says something to the other one. They both grab a big chunk of whatever they're eating, jam it in their mouth, and then dive down into the muck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we get up in the trees?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you'd like to try and climb them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we try and climb the trees?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're going to try and climb a tree.

preceding_turns.text: To what end? Then we're just gonna sit here forever?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well no, we can go from tree branch to tree branch. Are they tightly enough connected that we can travel on the top of the trees?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I am a backpack right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shit. Scanlan, do you want to ride on Grog's back still, or do you want to come up in the tree?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I go down and grab Scanlan and put him on the broom with me?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Honestly, I can't even tell the difference.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I know the twins are good on flying, for the most part.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As soon as you break up as well, you can see the large tree in the distance. Percy, doing the best you can to keep an eye on Grog or at least listen to that regard, you drink your potion of climbing and begin leaping off and kind of ninja-scattering your way through the higher branches of the trees as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This would be, for you it would be advantage, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

payload.speaker: MATT

To your surprise, as the potion energy surges through your body, even with the relatively slick surface that a lot of these branches and trees have, you have an innate ability to point out which ones are the strongest, easiest to grab a hold and just keep the momentum going in the semblance of a parkour-like leap-and-grab-type circumstance, you have managed to move through almost with the grace of the gorilla ahead of you. You lose sight a few times, but you guys do take the better part of the next forty minutes or so to eventually make your way closer and closer to the tree where the shadow, the shade of it against the permanently dusk sun now begins to creep over the place you are in the forest. You have now entered a shaded nightfall by the actual tree's location and placement. The lower forest below you is pitch-black at this point, other than a little bit of light that filters in from nearby lit areas of the forest. (Audible feedback.)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So if I can go ahead and get Grog and Percy to go ahead and make three successive athletics checks, if you could.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Um. For the purposes of climbing and grabbing, I would say this is more of an athletics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Since this is more upper-body strength and--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not parkouring, not parkouring through the treetops.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes you guys the better part of an hour to finally find a piece of abandoned dead tree husk from which you'll be able to carve a piece that matches the description of what Percy's requesting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're not on the ground, you're up in the actual tree top.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: While we still have a, I'll phone down to Vex and Vax and Percy and say, while we still have a giant eagle, is there any point to us, to me and Kiki scanning back to where those trolls were and just making sure they're not following us or anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, although if you find a log approximately two feet thick, I could certainly use that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: A log, about two feet thick.

preceding_turns.text: All right, so the two of you kind of-- make a perception check.

preceding_turns.text: From looking at it. It is muck.

preceding_turns.text: That's borderline Lisa Frank quotes here, this is...

payload.raw_text: You're trying desperately to finish up your short rest, as you get up, you hear this noise as well as you guys make your way to the front door. preceding_turns.text: Okay. If I reach and out touch one of the roots, like just run my hand along it, what happens?

preceding_turns.text: Sorry. I just love that word.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.text: Okay, glide backward, away from that large root that you had run into.

preceding_turns.text: Just the occasional shift, like a very faint movement that's rather sudden in certain areas nearby.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm gonna start making my way back to the mansion.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It was good, it was good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.text: The servants vanish and come back, you hear a familiar (dinging).

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.text: Before you beat this poor senseless cow--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) Pull her out, pull her out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And he just keeps pushing forward.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm scritching his ears while we walk.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. "You all come here, but one of you is the reason." It looks straight towards you. The light focuses and narrows from his eyes. "You. Sweet, broken Vex'ahlia. Unwanted daughter, unproven ally. Selfish and cruel. You drive those whom you would call family into danger and death for your own gain. I understand you." There's a softness and sadness to the face as he takes one step in your direction. "You are lost without form. Without knowing who you are, child. You stand shattered by expectation. Presenting an illusion of confidence. Behind it, you never left that bloodied girl, dagger in hand, body at your feet. You are a killer, run as you may. The tears you cry are not in vain, my sweet, my dear Vex'ahlia. You can rise above your trembling destiny. I have lived a thousand years and a day. I have seen many things. I can grant many gifts."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You all bow before this creature. Do you bow? All right.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: -- from the sides of the tree. Everyone roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Go ahead and roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have no respect for anyone who says we're not so different.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And shitting black diarrhea out of the tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the arrow hits his form as he's being pulled up-- (gasps)-- you hear the air escape like he had the wind knocked out of him. His glowing yellow eyes blink, blink, and his jaw kind of hangs open. There in the low-lit darkness. And you watch as more of that black, thick tar just begins to pour from his mouth onto the ground, splattering and seeping into the floor. His jaw slowly closes. "Then I am to be alone." He reaches his hand up, and you watch--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- as these vines reveal and give to him this large curved bow that he grasps in his hand. The other vines curl around his body, and you can see his form begin to build, like his actual body begins to grow as the vines become part of him. His face of flesh disappears behind an exterior of dark and gnarled wood. The voice grows deep as he all of a sudden withdraws into the side of the tree itself, and then bursts forth, landing on the ground. Not an elf-like entity, but some sort of gnarled root-like tree creature, holding Fenthras in one hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Was that bad? Should I have not attacked him?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, no, he was being terrible.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Become another memory here and keep me company in oblivion.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The two dead trees quake and begin to lift up--

preceding_turns.text: It looked like he took damage from it, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It didn't seem to catch fire, no. In fact, the decaying liquid it seems to be spewing out of it, it's almost--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's preventing any flame from catching on the creature in the vicinity as the flames slowly go out. The vines begin to reform to close up the ceiling, and you see Sondur is still kind of gliding through the vines and using them to move along the upper outside of the tree. Is that going to be the end of your movement?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll use a bonus action, gah, do I heal or do I inspire? What's more helpful here? I will heal Percy a little bit. I can only cast Healing Word at level one as a bonus action, right?

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you'll only have your bonus action left at that time, but essentially you climb up and get right up to where he is at the top of the tree. As he's up there pulling the bow back, getting ready to, you see another pointed arrow kind of shift out of his arm, you come right up to his face and you're right there holding on with the vines, with the axe hanging over your shoulder with the chain.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: With my bonus action, can I let out the loudest howl I possibly can.

preceding_turns.text: Yeah, come on! Please, I hope he can get in here.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yep. That ends his turn, it is now the treants' go. They both get up, using half their movement. (laughs) Let's see. That one currently is just being harried with the flames on the inside, it takes, does it take any damage on its turn?

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You pop out of his form as the living entity is no longer living and you are but an elemental now. I'm gonna leave him there just for reference, because it is a big thing to get through. But you are now pushed off to the side. That treant is gone. The other one gets up and steps over to Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

And then he's gonna go ahead and vine-walk into the wall. Ukurat does get an attack of opportunity on him. That does hit, a 27. So he takes 15 points of slashing damage. And vanishes back into the wall and emerges on this side here.

payload.raw_text: So you heal eight. You come to consciousness again on the ground, face-down, you can see the sludge sort of filling in your nostrils--
